Off Calendar Feb-07-2024 Continued To Apr-17-24 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION FOR LEAVE TO To File Second Amended Complaint; Memorandum Of Points & Authorities; & Declaration Of Steven L. Robinson (Renewed)
Off Calendar Feb-20-2024 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Atty. STEVEN L. ROBINSON, Esq. of Law Offices of Joseph L. Alioto and Angela Alioto (Tel:415-434-8700) for Plaintiff.
Atty. ROBERT ROGOYSKI, Esq. of San Francisco City Attorney's Office (Tel: 415-554-4284) for Defendant.

Hearing Re: PLAINTIFF KEITH BARAKA's MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File Second Amended Complaint.

Matter discussed. Counsel Robinson requests clarification. The Court adopts its tentative ruling with the modification to deny the motion without prejudice.

Plaintiff's motion for leave to file second amended complaint is denied. First, the motion is procedurally infirm. Plaintiff's moving papers do not contain a declaration in compliance with 3.1324(b). Second, the main relief that plaintiff seeks is to add a new wrongful termination claim that accrued years after he filed this action. The weight of authority states that it is improper to add a new claim in a supplemental complaint. (See Flood v. Simpson (1975) 45 Cal.App.3d 644, 647 [affirming denial of leave to supplement to add a new claim]; Hebert v. Los Angeles Raiders, Ltd. (1991) 23 Cal.App.4th 414, 426 [same]; Lee v. Bank of America (1994) 27 Cal.App.4th 197, 207 [criticizing plaintiff's authority (Honig v. Financial Corp. of America (1992) 6 Cal.App.4th 960)].

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not Reported. (302/RBU)

Off Calendar Dec-27-2023 Continued To Mar-06-24 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motiion Leave To File Second Amended Complaint; Memorandum Of Points & Authorities; Request For Judicial Notice; And Declaration Of Steven L. Robinson
Off Calendar Dec-12-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

RE: PLAINTIFF KEITH BARAKA's Motiion Leave To File Second Amended Complaint.

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel, or Interested Person.

The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: The "notice of motion and motion by plaintiff Keith Baraka for leave to file second amended complaint" is denied without prejudice. "A motion to amend a pleading before trial must (2) State what allegations to the pleading are proposed to be deleted, if any, and where, by page, paragraph, and line number, the deleted allegations are located; and (3) State what allegations are proposed to be added to the previous pleading, if any, and where, by page, paragraph, and line number, the additional allegations are located." (CRC 3.1324.)

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr. Clerk: Robert Woods. Not Reported. (302/RBU)
